mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate levels increase the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically reducing equity present values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS above 4% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, falsifying the discount-rate channel.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate that rises above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede weaker consumption and falling corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, refuting the labor-demand channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously falling industrial production and rising consumer prices generate a stagflationary impulse that compresses real profit margins and raises equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL increases fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy regimes defined by federal funds rates above 4%, any month-over-month increase in the policy rate tightens financial conditions abruptly, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns follow in more than 50% of observations despite further rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales directly captures consumer spending momentum, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining index of industrial production relative to total nonfarm payrolls indicates collapsing labor productivity, which squeezes corporate profit margins and foreshadows negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The ratio of INDPRO to PAYEMS fails to exhibit a statistically significant negative relationship with 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-stat < 2.0) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in nonfarm payroll employment reduce aggregate wage income and consumer demand expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as markets price in recession risk.
failure_condition: Back-to-back monthly PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts reflect strong construction activity and backward-linked demand for materials and labor, supporting broad economic growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1400, negative 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of observations, invalidating the construction-demand channel.
